data.current_summary: Open Topo Data API compatibility endpoint
data.current_description: Drop-in replacement for the Open Topo Data API.
data.summary_suggestion: Query elevation data via Open Topo Data API format
data.description_suggestion: Provides Open Topo Data API compatibility for elevation queries. Accepts location coordinates as query parameters and returns elevation data in Open Topo Data format. This endpoint enables seamless migration from Open Topo Data API to GPXZ without code changes. Supports single and multiple point elevation lookups.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague and doesn't explain what inputs are expected or what the response contains. AI agents need to understand the specific parameters (coordinates), the response format compatibility, and use cases. Added clarity about input parameters, output format, and the migration benefit.

data.current_summary: Open Topo Data API compatibility endpoint (POST)
data.current_description: Drop-in replacement for the Open Topo Data API via POST.
data.summary_suggestion: Query elevation data via Open Topo Data POST format
data.description_suggestion: Provides Open Topo Data API compatibility for elevation queries via POST method. Accepts location coordinates in the request body and returns elevation data in Open Topo Data format. This endpoint enables seamless migration from Open Topo Data API to GPXZ without code changes. Supports batch elevation lookups for multiple points.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about request body format, response structure, and use cases. AI agents need to understand when to use POST vs GET, what data structure to send, and what to expect in return. Added clarity about request body parameters, response format, and batch processing capability.

data.current_description: Retrieves elevation data for multiple geographic coordinates. Supports up to 512 points.

data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks detail about the response format and structure. Added information about return value (array of results) to help AI agents understand what data they will receive.

data.current_description: Returns elevation at evenly-spaced intervals along a route. Supports up to 5,000 input points and returns between 2 and 512 sampled points.

data.current_summary: Google Maps Elevation API compatibility endpoint
data.current_description: Drop-in replacement for the Google Maps Elevation API. Supports both positional and sampled elevation queries.
data.description_suggestion: Drop-in replacement for the Google Maps Elevation API. Supports both positional elevation queries (single/multiple points) and sampled elevation queries (along paths). Accepts Google Maps API-compatible parameters and returns responses in the same format. Useful for migrating from Google Maps Elevation API.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but could be more action-oriented. Description lacks detail about what types of queries are supported and what the compatibility means in practice. Added clarification about query types, parameter compatibility, and migration use case to improve AI agent understanding.
